reasoning: The Brooklyn Nets are currently in the early stages of a massive rebuild after trading away Mikal Bridges and reacquiring their draft picks. They are widely projected to be one of the worst teams in the Eastern Conference for the remainder of the 2024-25 season and looking ahead to the 2025-26 season. Even if they hit a home run in the 2025 draft (e.g., Cooper Flagg) and sign high-profile free agents, going from a bottom-dwelling team to winning the Eastern Conference Finals in a single season is extremely rare in the NBA. Given their current roster composition, timeline, and conference competition (Celtics, Knicks, 76ers, Bucks, etc.), the probability of them winning the Eastern Conference Finals in 2025-26 is very close to zero.
